title: HTML and JavaScript Integration Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential methods for adding behavior and interactivity to your web pages using JavaScript.

questions.question: Which tag is used to integrate JavaScript into an HTML document?
What is the primary benefit of using the 'defer' attribute on an external <script> tag?
options: It ensures the script is executed ONLY after the entire HTML document is parsed.
It hides the script from search engines.
It executes the script before any HTML is parsed.

questions.question: Which attribute on a <script> tag downloads the script in the background and executes it as soon as it's ready, potentially before the HTML is finished parsing?
Traditionally, where is the most common place to put internal <script> tags to ensure the HTML loads first?
Which of these is an example of an 'event handler' that can trigger JavaScript directly from an HTML attribute?
